items.q: What is the main advantage of asymmetric encryption?
items.correct: Secure key exchange without sharing a secret in advance.
items.misconception: It is faster than symmetric encryption.
items.why: Students think asymmetric is just 'better'. It's actually SLOWER than symmetric. Its advantage is solving key distribution - the public key can be shared openly without compromising security.

items.q: What does in-order traversal of a binary search tree produce?
items.misconception: Nodes in the order they were inserted.
items.why: Students forget the BST property: left subtree less than node less than right subtree. In-order (left, node, right) therefore visits values in sorted order - regardless of insertion order.

items.q: Why is UDP used for live video streaming despite being unreliable?
items.correct: Lower overhead and latency - retransmitting late packets would harm real-time quality.
items.misconception: Because TCP cannot handle video data.
items.random_wrong: Because UDP is always faster than TCP.
items.why: Students assume reliability is always desirable. For real-time media, a dropped packet is preferable to a delayed one - UDP trades reliability for timeliness, which suits live media.
Correct answer: Microsoft Word. “Linux kernel” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many main categories of software are taught at GCSE (system and application)?
items.why: The real figure is 2 categories. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
